matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules also receive the actual sample REF -> ALT change; curated allele-change rules take precedence when the observed ALT allele or exact change has a known direction.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an AMPK energy-sensing thesis: the individual may carry a low-penetrance modifier of gastric-cancer association studies, metabolic stress adaptation, mitochondrial response, or mTOR restraint because PRKAA1 encodes the AMPK alpha-1 catalytic subunit. This is a pathway-risk context rather than a deterministic disease prediction.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: Observed rs13361707 suggests this sample carries the PRKAA1 prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests an AMPK energy-sensing thesis: the individual may carry a low-penetrance modifier of gastric-cancer association studies, metabolic stress adaptation, mitochondrial response, or mTOR restraint because PRKAA1 encodes the AMPK alpha-1 catalytic subunit. This is a pathway-risk context rather than a deterministic disease prediction. This specific marker is curated as gastric-cancer-associated PRKAA1 SNP. rs13361707 is the best-established common PRKAA1 marker in gastric-cancer association literature. The local database treats it as a low-penetrance pathway-associated risk marker rather than as a pathogenic clinical allele.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs13361707 with the actual DNA change {change}. Anchor the PRKAA1 prediction to that observed REF -> ALT result instead of treating the variant as only a theoretical marker. The current preview does not infer zygosity or allele dose, so dosage-specific conclusions require genotype fields.

allele_change_rules.prediction: This sample row reports C as the observed alternate allele at PRKAA1 rs13361707. The bundled gastric-cancer association study reports the C allele as the higher-risk direction in an eastern Chinese population, so ALT C supports a low-penetrance AMPK-pathway risk-context thesis. Do not treat this as diagnostic without ancestry, phenotype, and genotype-dose context.
allele_change_rules.basis: Observed ALT C at rs13361707, the PRKAA1 allele highlighted in local gastric-cancer susceptibility evidence.

evidence.label: PubMed 26485766: PRKAA1 rs13361707 and gastric-cancer risk
PubMed 37670284: PRKAA1 polymorphisms and gastric cancer

allele_change_rules.prediction: This sample row reports T as the observed alternate allele at PRKAA1 rs13361707. T is the contrast state to the C-linked gastric-cancer risk direction in the bundled evidence, so the prediction should remain an AMPK-pathway context signal rather than a C-allele risk statement.
allele_change_rules.basis: Observed ALT T at rs13361707, interpreted as the contrast state to the C-linked gastric-cancer evidence.

evidence.label: PubMed 26485766: PRKAA1 rs13361707 and gastric-cancer risk
PubMed 37670284: PRKAA1 polymorphisms and gastric cancer

variant_prediction_rules.basis: Likely acts through regulatory or linked-locus effects on AMPK-pathway signaling rather than as a direct protein-disrupting lesion.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an AMPK energy-sensing thesis: the individual may carry a low-penetrance modifier of gastric-cancer association studies, metabolic stress adaptation, mitochondrial response, or mTOR restraint because PRKAA1 encodes the AMPK alpha-1 catalytic subunit. This is a pathway-risk context rather than a deterministic disease prediction. An observed PRKAA1 variant places this sample in the curated PRKAA1 research context. The local PRKAA1 knowledge base is built around metabolic and cancer-association literature. Common PRKAA1 variants are best interpreted as pathway modifiers rather than as highly penetrant clinical alleles. Common PRKAA1 variants are typically interpreted as low-penetrance metabolic or cancer-risk modifiers.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: PRKAA1 is central to AMPK signaling, nutrient sensing, mitochondrial stress responses, and mTOR restraint.
The strongest common-variant literature centers on gastric-cancer susceptibility and metabolic-endocrine phenotypes.
Energy-stress and nutrient-sensing biology.
